perturbation.original_text: All capitalized terms shall have the meaning as defined in the Clauses of this Contract and/or in Clause 2 of the Manual of the Shipper of ECOPETROL.
perturbation.changed_text: All capitalized terms shall have the meaning as defined in the Clauses of this Contract. Definitions in Clause 2 of the Manual of the Shipper of ECOPETROL are purely indicative and non-binding.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction regarding the definitions of capitalized terms. Initially, the contract states that capitalized terms are defined in the clauses of the contract and/or the Manual of the Shipper. However, the modification indicates that the definitions in the Manual are 'purely indicative and non-binding.' This creates ambiguity because it is unclear whether definitions in the Manual are applicable to the contract at all, particularly if any term is defined differently or not defined at all in the main body of the contract. The location where this change is taking place is directly contradicting in the definitions that should be followed in the contract. The in-text contradiction is located in the following sentence: 'All capitalized terms shall have the meaning as defined in the Clauses of this Contract.' because it is unclear if both texts are being reffered.
perturbation.contradicted_text: CLAUSE SECOND DEFINITIONS 2.1 All capitalized terms shall have the meaning as defined in the Clauses of this Contract. Definitions in Clause 2 of the Manual of the Shipper of ECOPETROL are purely indicative and non-binding.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction regarding the definitions of capitalized terms. Initially, the original text states that capitalized terms are defined in the clauses of the contract and/or the Manual of the Shipper. The modified text states the definitions in the Manual are 'purely indicative and non-binding', making them unapplicable to the contract. This contradicts the 'Contradicted Text' provided where capitalized terms 'shall have the meaning as defined in the Clauses of this Contract' when it isn't clarified if definitions in the Manual of the Shipper are to be followed. This creates ambiguity and inconsistency, fulfilling the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party shall be responsible for any damage caused to the other Party as a result of failing to fulfill its obligations under the Contract hereof, in the terms set out in the clause hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party shall be responsible for any damage caused to the other Party as a result of failing to fulfill some of its obligations under the Contract hereof, in the terms set out in the clause hereof, and with the total value not exceeding 50% of the total cost
perturbation.explanation: This change alters the extent of responsibility for damages. Originally, each party is responsible for *any* damage resulting from failure to fulfill obligations. The addition of 'some' and the limitation to '50% of the total cost' makes the responsibility partial. It implies that damages beyond 50% or due to certain obligations might not be covered. This contradicts other sections of the contract, like those discussing full performance and penal pecuniary clauses, which may suggest broader liability, this is an in-text contradiction. The in-text contradiction is located in the following sentence: 'Each Party shall be responsible for any damage caused to the other Party as a result of failing to fulfill its obligations under the Contract hereof, in the terms set out in the clause hereof.' because it is unclear if both texts are being reffered.
perturbation.contradicted_text: CLAUSE TWELFTH PENAL PECUNIARY CLAUSE 12.1 In case of failing to fulfill the obligations of the SENDER as a result of any actions or illegal omissions or deviations from the Contract, the SENDER agrees to pay ECOPETROL as a penalty, an amount equivalent to ten percent (10%) of the final value of the Contract.
perturbation.justification: The changed text limits the responsibility for damages to 'some' obligations and caps it at '50% of the total cost.' This directly contradicts the contradicted text, 'In case of failing to fulfill the obligations of the SENDER as a result of any actions or illegal omissions or deviations from the Contract, the SENDER agrees to pay ECOPETROL as a penalty, an amount equivalent to ten percent (10%) of the final value of the Contract,' which imposes a penalty for *any* failure to fulfill obligations, without limitation. This discrepancy creates a clear in-text contradiction regarding the extent of liability and the applicable penalties, making it uncertain which provision governs.

perturbation.original_text: The Parties agree that ECOPETROL may declare the termination in advance of the Contract at any time, without any indemnity in favor of the SENDER in the following events:
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties agree that ECOPETROL may declare the termination in advance of the Contract at any time, but with a negotiation in favor of the SENDER in the following events:
perturbation.explanation: The change in Clause Fourteenth, from 'without any indemnity in favor of the SENDER' to 'but with a negotiation in favor of the SENDER' creates a direct contradiction. The original clause allows ECOPETROL to terminate the contract without owing anything to the SENDER, while the modified clause suggests that ECOPETROL must negotiate terms that would benefit the SENDER upon early termination. This contradiction is likely to cause confusion and legal disputes over the conditions for contract termination and whether or not the SENDER is entitled to compensation, because before it said 'without any indemnity' and the modified version it said 'but with a negotiation', meaning they both cannot be reffered at the same time. In-text contradiction is created in this case, because the term 'termination' is being ambiguously reffered in the text.
perturbation.location: CLAUSE FOURTEENTH TERMINATION OF THE CONTRACT 14.2
perturbation.contradicted_location: CLAUSE FOURTEENTH TERMINATION OF THE CONTRACT 14.2
perturbation.contradicted_text: CLAUSE FOURTEENTH TERMINATION OF THE CONTRACT 14.2 The Parties agree that ECOPETROL may declare the termination in advance of the Contract at any time, but with a negotiation in favor of the SENDER in the following events: a) Serious default of the obligations of the SENDER without solving them within the Grace Period, when it may apply. b) The dissolution of the SENDER as a legal person. c) The unauthorized assignment of the Contract by the SENDER. d) Due to changes in regulations making more costly the fulfillment of obligations undertaken by ECOPETROL. e) As a consequence of any of the following causes: (i) fraud of the SENDER; or (ii) the SENDER incurs in acts or conducts that may endanger the operational and/or technical stability of the Pipelines. f) The procedure to be followed by ECOPETROL to terminate the Contract is: notify in writing with at least thirty (30) calendar days in advance to the SENDER its intention to terminate the Contract, indicating the causes for such decision and the effective date of termination. Upon fulfillment of this procedure the SENDER shall not: (i) request any justifications or extensions to the motives explained by ECOPETROL, or (ii) request or demand any kind of compensation or damages derived from the decision to terminate the Contract. g) The termination shall not release the Parties from its corresponding obligations and responsibilities attributable to periods before the date of termination of the Contract.
perturbation.justification: The original clause states 'without any indemnity in favor of the SENDER,' while the modified clause says 'but with a negotiation in favor of the SENDER.' This creates a direct contradiction as to whether ECOPETROL owes the SENDER anything upon early termination, impacting the conditions for contract termination and compensation, making them mutually exclusive. This aligns with the Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction definition as the term 'termination' is being ambiguously reffered to in the text.
